personas.name: Operator
personas.role: Deploying and Managing a Liminal Server

stories.text: As an Operator, I want to start a liminal server with a single config file so that I can deploy the messaging bus without writing Rust code.
As an Operator, I want to define channels, routing rules, and persistence paths in the config file so that I can set up the bus topology declaratively.
As an Operator, I want the server to validate my configuration at startup and report specific errors so that I can fix misconfigurations before the server accepts traffic.
As an Operator, I want to shut down the server gracefully so that in-flight conversations complete and durable state is persisted before the process exits.
As an Operator, I want to form a cluster of liminal servers via seed nodes so that I can scale horizontally and survive individual node failures.
As an Operator, I want to check server health via HTTP so that I can integrate with monitoring systems and alerting pipelines.
As a DevOps Engineer, I want to configure the server via environment variables so that I can deploy with container orchestration tools that inject config through the environment.
As a DevOps Engineer, I want a readiness probe endpoint that reports ready only after full startup so that Kubernetes does not route traffic to a server that cannot yet accept connections.
As a DevOps Engineer, I want the server to run as a single static binary so that container images are minimal and deployment artifacts are straightforward to distribute.

As a DevOps Engineer, I want environment variables to override file-based config values so that I can ship a base config in the container image and apply per-environment overrides from the orchestrator.
